plan: Step 1: Create `src/styles/share-button.css` — Style the share button matching existing info-button pattern. Use `position: absolute; bottom: 80px; right: 24px` to stack above the info button (at bottom: 24px). Use identical 44px circular design: `width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 50%; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.3); background: rgba(0,0,0,0.5); color: #fff; pointer-events: auto; cursor: pointer`. Add hover/focus-visible states matching info-button. Use a share icon via Unicode character ↗ (U+2197) or CSS-drawn arrow.

Step 2: Create `src/styles/toast.css` — Toast notification styled as a fixed-position pill. Position at `bottom: 160px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%)` to center horizontally and sit well above the button stack (info at ~24px, share at ~80px, top edge of share at ~124px — 160px gives 36px clearance). Style: `background: rgba(0,0,0,0.8); color: #fff; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 14px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; pointer-events: none`. Use opacity/visibility transition (0.3s ease) with a `.toast--visible` modifier class. Add a `.toast--error` variant with a subtle red-tinted background `rgba(180,40,40,0.85)`.

Step 3: Create `src/ui/toast.ts` — Export `createToast(container: HTMLElement): Toast` where `Toast` has a `show(message: string, type?: 'success' | 'error'): void` method. Implementation: create a `<div class="toast">` element, append to container. `show()` sets textContent, toggles `toast--visible` and optionally `toast--error` classes, and uses `setTimeout` (2500ms) to auto-hide by removing `toast--visible`. If a toast is already visible when `show()` is called again, clear the previous timeout and restart.

Step 4: Create `src/ui/share-button.ts` — Export `createShareButton(container: HTMLElement): HTMLButtonElement`. Create a `<button class="share-button" aria-label="Share this page">` with textContent '↗'. Append to container and return the element. Pure DOM factory — no event logic here.

Step 5: Create `src/ui/share-action.ts` — Export `async function performShare(url: string): Promise<{ ok: boolean; method: 'share' | 'clipboard' | 'none' }>`. Implementation: (1) If `navigator.share` exists, try `await navigator.share({ title: '404 Museum', url })`. On success return `{ ok: true, method: 'share' }`. Catch errors: if `error instanceof DOMException && error.name === 'AbortError'`, the user cancelled the share sheet — return `{ ok: true, method: 'share' }` (silent no-op, no fallback). For any other share error, fall through to clipboard. (2) Clipboard fallback: if `navigator.clipboard?.writeText` exists, try `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(url)`, return `{ ok: true, method: 'clipboard' }`. On failure return `{ ok: false, method: 'none' }`. (3) If neither API available, return `{ ok: false, method: 'none' }`.

Step 6: Wire everything in `src/main.ts` — Add CSS imports: `import './styles/share-button.css'` and `import './styles/toast.css'`. Add module imports: `createShareButton` from `./ui/share-button`, `createToast` from `./ui/toast`, `performShare` from `./ui/share-action`. Inside the `if (overlayUi)` block, after the info button setup: create share button via `createShareButton(overlayUi)`, create toast via `createToast(overlayUi)`. Add click handler on share button: call `performShare(window.location.href)`, then based on result — if `ok && method === 'clipboard'` show toast 'Link copied to clipboard', if `ok && method === 'share'` do nothing (native share UI or user cancelled both need no extra feedback), if `!ok` show error toast 'Could not share link'.

Step 7: Create `tests/us-015-share-button.test.ts` — Follow the established readFileSync + regex test pattern from US-013 (NOT jsdom). Structure: (A) 'source files exist' — assert existence of `src/ui/share-button.ts`, `src/ui/share-action.ts`, `src/ui/toast.ts`, `src/styles/share-button.css`, `src/styles/toast.css`. (B) 'share-button.css' — regex tests for `pointer-events: auto`, `bottom:` and `right:` positioning, `position: (fixed|absolute)`, min touch target 44px (reuse extractCssBlock helper). (C) 'toast.css' — regex for `pointer-events: none` (non-interactive), opacity or visibility transition, bottom value >= 140 (sufficient clearance from buttons), `.toast--visible` class exists, `.toast--error` variant exists. (D) 'share-button.ts' — regex for `export.*createShareButton`, `aria-label`, `share-button` class name. (E) 'share-action.ts' — regex for `navigator.share`, `navigator.clipboard`, `AbortError` (confirms explicit handling), export of `performShare`. (F) 'toast.ts' — regex for `export.*createToast`, `toast--visible`, `setTimeout`. (G) 'main.ts integration' — regex for imports of share-button, toast, share-action modules and their CSS files. (H) 'build verification' — `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` pass.
Step 8: Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to validate everything passes.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test` — all tests in us-015-share-button.test.ts must pass, and existing tests (us-005, us-013) must not regress.
Run `npm run typecheck` — zero TypeScript errors across all new and existing files.
Run `npm run lint` — zero ESLint violations in src/ directory.
Run `npm run build` — Vite production build succeeds without errors.

Manual browser test: open dev server, verify share button appears above info button in bottom-right corner, click it — on desktop (no Web Share API) it should copy URL to clipboard and show 'Link copied' toast. Toast should appear centered horizontally, well above the button stack with no visual overlap.

Manual mobile test (or Chrome DevTools mobile emulation with Web Share API): click share button triggers native share sheet. Cancelling the share sheet should produce no toast or error — silent no-op.
Verify the URL in clipboard/share payload contains the ?seed= parameter.
risks: Web Share API availability varies: fully supported on mobile Safari/Chrome, limited on desktop browsers. The clipboard fallback covers desktop, but navigator.clipboard.writeText requires a secure context (HTTPS or localhost) — will fail on plain HTTP. This is acceptable since the project targets static hosting (Vercel) which serves HTTPS.

Toast z-index must be high enough to appear above all generated site content but the #overlay-ui container already has z-index: 10, so elements inside it will naturally layer above #generated-site (z-index: 1). No explicit z-index needed on the toast itself.

The readFileSync + regex test approach validates code structure but not runtime behavior. This is a known tradeoff — the build verification tests (tsc + vite build) catch type errors and import issues, while the regex tests confirm the required patterns exist in source. Runtime correctness is verified via manual testing and future E2E tests.
